ETLDelphi 0.1.0

    • run_etl: Detailed Steps and Mechanics

Extract structured data from SOAP notes and populate note_nlp

Source: R/extract_note_nlp.R
extract_note_nlp.Rd

Parses SOAP notes into sections, extracts clinical entities (conditions, prescriptions, procedures, screenings) using regex, maps them to OMOP concepts via vocabulary lookup and LLM, then loads into cdm.note_nlp and supplements cdm.condition_occurrence, cdm.drug_exposure, and cdm.procedure_occurrence.

extract_note_nlp(
  con,
  config = NULL,
  log_path = "note_nlp_mapping_log.csv",
  provider = NULL,
  model = NULL,
  api_key = NULL,
  hecate = NULL,
  skip_llm = FALSE,
  skip_load = FALSE
)

Arguments

con

DBI connection to the DuckDB database (post-ETL).

config

ETL config list. Default: default_etl_config().

log_path

Path for the concept mapping log CSV.

provider

LLM provider for unmatched entities: "anthropic" or "openai". Default: auto-detected.

model

LLM model name. Default: from env var.

api_key

LLM API key. Default: from env var.

hecate

Hecate client object. Default: created from env vars.

skip_llm

If TRUE, only use vocabulary lookup (no LLM calls).

skip_load

If TRUE, only do concept mapping (don't load CDM tables).

Value

Invisible data.frame of mapping results.

Pipeline integration


# 1. Run ETL (includes note parsing + entity extraction in 45_nlp step)
run_etl(con)

# 2. Map entities and load note_nlp + supplemental facts
extract_note_nlp(con)

Contents
